metadata.split_role: Expanded Suillus and Leccinum/scaber-stalk bolete records.
metadata.notes: Physical data split only. Species record bodies were preserved; each canonical slug appears in exactly one base mushroom file. Updated in v4.2.82 with 25-record mushroom credibility pass including Black Velvet Bolete and Blue-staining Slippery Jack. v4.2.83: mushroom bolete photo/credibility batch; Commons images added for Neoporphyrellus alboater and Suillus tomentosus; 25-record Suillus/Leccinum/Neoporphyrellus pass. v4.2.85 refreshed Blue-staining Slippery Jack image URLs to direct upload.wikimedia.org files and carried forward v4.2.83 image coverage. v4.2.86: optional photo patches are treated as fill-only for existing base-record image fields; direct image fallback hardened for Black Velvet Bolete and Suillus tomentosus where applicable. v4.2.87-r2026-04-30-mushroom-suillus-leccinum-credibility4: 25-record Suillus/Leccinum credibility pass; source/species-note issues reduced while image/local/taxonomy reviews were retained. v4.2.99-r2026-05-11-fold-mushroom-photo-patches1: folded mushroom photo patch records directly into canonical source records and retired runtime PHOTO_PATCH_PATHS. v4.3.0-r2026-05-11-data-chunk-size-cleanup1 split active data sources by size; record bodies and source order preserved.

review_reason_audit.rule: Normalize explicit review signals into review_reasons/review_note and prefix notes for Needs Review list visibility.

metadata.split_note: v4.3.0-r2026-05-11-data-chunk-size-cleanup1 split this active data source by file size to keep working chunks under roughly 75–100 KB. Record bodies and source order were preserved.

mushroom_profile.summary: Dotted-stalk Suillus: eastern white pine associate with brown young pores and dense darkening glandular dots.
mushroom_profile.season_note: Summer and fall with eastern white pine.
research_notes: Species seed added from the Michigan bolete audit.
Needs source links and fuller field notes.
Look under eastern white pine for a slimy yellow to yellow-brown cap, brown young pore surface, and a stem densely dotted with glandular spots that darken with age.
Source/species-note pass applied in v4.2.87-r2026-04-30-mushroom-suillus-leccinum-credibility4.
pore_color: Brown to orange-brown when young; yellowish to olive with age

records.edibility_detail: Edible reputation is modest; cook well and expect typical Suillus texture/slime issues.
records.field_identification: Look under eastern white pine for a slimy yellow to yellow-brown cap, brown young pore surface, and a stem densely dotted with glandular spots that darken with age.

records.mushroom_card_note: Yellow/orange Suillus that may occur with aspens, oaks, or pine; ringless, glandular-dotted, and often red-streaked.
records.field_identification: Look for yellow cap with red streaks/spots, orange young pores, ringless stem with glandular dots, reddish staining in flesh/stem base, and hardwood/aspen/oak association.
records.edibility_detail: Not a beginner food target, but useful to know because it breaks the “Suillus equals conifer” rule.

records.notes: White Suillus: a pale sticky Suillus strongly associated with eastern white pine. Look for the whitish cap/stem and contrasting glandular dots rather than treating every pale Suillus as this species.

mushroom_profile.summary: White Suillus: eastern white pine associate with bald white cap and conspicuous glandular dots/smears on a slender stem.
staining: May slowly stain pinkish to reddish when sliced
